---
title: "Overview and workflow"
subtitle: "A Python port of the fmrireg overview vignette."
jupyter: python3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

## Core Workflow

`fmrimod` combines timing information, HRF specification, design-matrix
construction, and matrix GLM utilities. A typical workflow starts with an
event table and an acquisition grid.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import fmrimod as fm
from fmrimod.design_colmap import design_colmap

events = pd.DataFrame({
    "onset": [0, 12, 24, 36, 48, 60],
    "stim_type": ["face", "scene", "face", "scene", "face", "scene"],
})

sf = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=[40, 40], TR=1.0)
sf.blocklens
```

## Event Model

The formula creates one HRF-convolved regressor per stimulus level.

```{python}
model = fm.event_model(
    'onset ~ hrf(stim_type, basis="spmg1")',
    data=events,
    sampling_frame=fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=80, TR=1.0),
)

model.design_matrix.shape
```

```{python}
design_colmap(model)[["col", "name", "condition", "basis_name"]]
```

## Baseline and Contrasts

Baseline modeling and contrast testing are separate decisions. The design
matrix stores task regressors; contrast vectors state the scientific
comparison in that column space.

```{python}
columns = model.column_names
face_vs_scene = np.zeros(len(columns))
face_vs_scene[columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.face")] = 1
face_vs_scene[columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.scene")] = -1

pd.DataFrame({"column": columns, "face_vs_scene": face_vs_scene})
```

## Matrix GLM

For a compact demonstration, simulate five voxel time series directly from the
design matrix, then fit ordinary least squares.

```{python}
rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
X = np.asarray(model.design_matrix)
true_beta = np.array([[1.0, 0.8, 0.6, 0.4, 0.2],
                      [-0.2, -0.1, 0.0, 0.1, 0.2]])
Y = X @ true_beta + rng.normal(scale=0.08, size=(X.shape[0], 5))

fit = fm.fmri_ols_fit(Y, X)
fit["beta"].shape, fit["t"].shape
```

## Advanced Topics

The same design/data contract supports sketched solvers, AR diagnostics,
single-trial estimation, benchmark fixtures, and group-level meta-analysis.
Those topics are covered in the companion tutorials.

## Next

- [Linear model](linear-model.qmd)
- [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd)
- [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd)
